title: Mid session 2901 T225 B
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2025

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test B

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 2

1. [10 marks] A bag contains red, blue, and green balls in equal proportions. A ball is drawn n times independently with replacement (meaning, we put each ball back in the bag before the next draw). Let X be the number of red balls drawn, Y be the number of blue balls drawn, and Z be the number of green balls drawn.

a) [5 marks] Find the covariance between X and Y .

(Hint: Write X = ni=1 Xi and Y = nj=1 Yj , where Xi = 1I{red at the ith draw} P P and Yj = 1I{blue at the j th draw} )

b) [2 marks] The covariance that you obtain in a) should be negative. Explain intuitively why.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 3

c) [3 marks] Find the variance of the total number of red or blue balls drawn; that is, Var(X + Y ).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 4

2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a bivariate discrete vector, with SX1 = {1, 2, 3}, SX2 = {1, 2, 3} and pX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = c(x1 +x2 ) for (x1 , x2 ) ∈ SX1 X2 = {(x1 , x2 ) ∈ SX1 × SX2 : x1 + x2 ≤ 4} and a constant c > 0.

a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 5

c) [3 marks] Find E(X1 ).

d) [2 marks] Find Cov(X1 , X2 ).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 6

3. [10 marks] A manufacturing process produces items which are defective with probability π = 0.02, independently of each other. We inspect n = 400 items from this process. Let X be the number of defective items found.

a) [6 marks] Use the Central Limit Theorem to approximate the probability P(5 ≤ X ≤ 15).

b) [4 marks] On a particular day, 40 defective items are found among the 400 inspected. Based on your CLT approximation, would you consider this outcome consistent with the statement π = 0.02? Justify your answer with an appropriate probability calculation.

(Hint: Φ(0.3826) = 0.6490, Φ(0.8928) = 0.8140, Φ(1.0714) = 0.8580, Φ(1.25) = 0.8944, Φ(2.5) = 0.9938, Φ(2.6786) = 0.9963, Φ(5) ≈ 1)
